For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL> command do?

The copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL> command copies an existing checkpoint configuration to a USB drive. The file format is defined when the checkpoint was created. Syntax: copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL> command?
Syntax: copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy <REMOTE-URL> checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command do?

The copy <REMOTE-URL> checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command copies a remote configuration file to a checkpoint. The remote configuration file must be in checkpoint format. Syntax: copy <REMOTE-URL> checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy <REMOTE-URL> checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command?
Syntax: copy <REMOTE-URL> checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy <REMOTE-URL> {running-config startup-config} command?
Syntax: copy <REMOTE-URL> {running-config | startup-config } [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy running-config {startup-config checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME>} command?
Syntax: copy running-config {startup-config | checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME>}
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy {running-config startup-config} <REMOTE-URL> command?
Syntax: copy {running-config | startup-config} <REMOTE-URL> {cli | json} [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy {running-config startup-config} <STORAGE-URL> command?
Syntax: copy {running-config | startup-config} <STORAGE-URL> {cli | json}
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy startup-config running-config command do?
The copy startup-config running-config command copies the startup configuration to the running configuration. Syntax: copy startup-config running-config.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy startup-config running-config command?
Syntax: copy startup-config running-config
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy <STORAGE-URL> running-config command?
Syntax: copy <STORAGE-URL> {running-config | startup-config | checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME>}
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erase {checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> startup-config all} command?
Syntax: erase {checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> | startup-config | all}
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command do?
The show checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command shows the configuration of a checkpoint. Syntax: show checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> [json].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command?
Syntax: show checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> [json]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show checkpoint list command do?
The show checkpoint list command shows a list of saved checkpoints. Syntax: show checkpoint list {all | <START-DATE> <END-DATE>}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show checkpoint list command?
Syntax: show checkpoint list {all | <START-DATE> <END-DATE>}
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the boot set-default command do?
The boot set-default command sets the default operating system image to use when the system is booted. Syntax: boot set-default {primary | secondary}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the boot set-default command?
Syntax: boot set-default {primary | secondary}
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the boot system command do?

The boot system command reboots all modules on the switch. By default, the configured default operating system image is used. Optional parameters enable you to specify which system image to use for the reboot operation and for future reboot operations. Syntax: boot system [primary | secondary | serviceos].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the boot system command?
Syntax: boot system [primary | secondary | serviceos]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy {primary secondary} <REMOTE-URL> command do?
The copy {primary secondary} <REMOTE-URL> command uploads a firmware image to a TFTP or SFTP server. Syntax: copy {primary | secondary} <REMOTE-URL> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy {primary secondary} <REMOTE-URL> command?
Syntax: copy {primary | secondary} <REMOTE-URL> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy {primary secondary} <FIRMWARE-FILENAME> command do?
The copy {primary secondary} <FIRMWARE-FILENAME> command copies a firmware image to USB storage. 100 AOS-CX 10.06 Fundamentals Guide. Syntax: copy {primary | secondary} <FIRMWARE-FILENAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy {primary secondary} <FIRMWARE-FILENAME> command?
Syntax: copy {primary | secondary} <FIRMWARE-FILENAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy primary secondary command do?
The copy primary secondary command copies the firmware image from the primary to the secondary location. Syntax: copy primary secondary.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy primary secondary command?
Syntax: copy primary secondary
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy <REMOTE-URL> command do?
The copy <REMOTE-URL> command downloads and installs a firmware image from a TFTP or SFTP server. Syntax: copy <REMOTE-URL> {primary | secondary} [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy <REMOTE-URL> command?
Syntax: copy <REMOTE-URL> {primary | secondary} [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy secondary primary command do?
The copy secondary primary command copies the firmware image from the secondary to the primary location. Syntax: copy secondary primary.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy secondary primary command?
Syntax: copy secondary primary
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy <STORAGE-URL> command?
Syntax: copy <STORAGE-URL> {primary | secondary}
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure SNMP?

To configure SNMP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable SNMP on a VRF using the command snmp-server vrf default 2. Set the system contact, location, and description for the switch with the following commands: 3. If required, change the default SNMP port on which the agent listens for requests with the command. Commands shown in the procedure include: snmp-server agent-port..
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the rmon alarm index <INDEX> snmp-oid <SNMP-OID> rising-threshold <RISING-THRESHOLD> command do?

The rmon alarm index <INDEX> snmp-oid <SNMP-OID> rising-threshold <RISING-THRESHOLD> command stores configuration entries in an alarm table that defines the sample interval, sample-type, and threshold parameters for an SNMP MIB object. Only the SNMP MIB objects that resolve to an ASN.1 primitive type of INTEGER (INTEGER, Integer32, Counter32, Counter64, Gauge32, or Time Ticks) will be monitored. The no form of this command removes all RMON alarms and allows you to specify an index to remove a particular RMON alarm. Syntax: rmon alarm index <INDEX> snmp-oid <SNMP-OID> rising-threshold <RISING-THRESHOLD>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the rmon alarm index <INDEX> snmp-oid <SNMP-OID> rising-threshold <RISING-THRESHOLD> command?
Syntax: rmon alarm index <INDEX> snmp-oid <SNMP-OID> rising-threshold <RISING-THRESHOLD>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show rmon alarm command do?
The show rmon alarm command displays the RMON alarm configurations. Syntax: show rmon alarm [index <INDEX>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show rmon alarm command?
Syntax: show rmon alarm [index <INDEX>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmp agent-port command do?
The show snmp agent-port command displays SNMP agent UDP port number. Syntax: show snmp agent-port.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmp agent-port command?
Syntax: show snmp agent-port
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmp community command do?
The show snmp community command displays a list of all configured SNMP v1/v2c communities. Syntax: show snmp community.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmp community command?
Syntax: show snmp community
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmp system command do?
The show snmp system command displays SNMP description, location, and contact information. Syntax: show snmp system.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmp system command?
Syntax: show snmp system
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmp trap command do?
The show snmp trap command displays all configured SNMP traps/informs receivers. Syntax: show snmp trap.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmp trap command?
Syntax: show snmp trap
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmp vrf command do?
The show snmp vrf command displays the VRF on which the SNMP agent service is running. Syntax: show snmp vrf.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmp vrf command?
Syntax: show snmp vrf
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmpv3 context command do?
The show snmpv3 context command displays all configured SNMP contexts. Syntax: show snmpv3 context.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 context command?
Syntax: show snmpv3 context
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmpv3 engine-id command do?

The show snmpv3 engine-id command displays the configured SNMP v3 snmp engine-id. If the SNMP v3 engine-id is not configured, by default a unique engine-id is created by the switch using a combination of the enterprise OID value and the switch's MAC address. Syntax: show snmpv3 engine-id.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 engine-id command?
Syntax: show snmpv3 engine-id
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmpv3 security-level command do?
The show snmpv3 security-level command displays the configured SNMP v3 security level. Syntax: show snmpv3 security-level.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 security-level command?
Syntax: show snmpv3 security-level
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmpv3 users command do?
The show snmpv3 users command displays all configured SNMP v3 users. For more details on the user enabled status, see snmpv3 security-level. Syntax: show snmpv3 users.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 users command?
Syntax: show snmpv3 users
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server agent-port command do?

The snmp-server agent-port command sets the UDP port number that the SNMP master agent uses to communicate. UDP port 161 is the default port. The no form of this command sets the SNMP master agent port to the default value. Syntax: snmp-server agent-port <PORT>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server agent-port command?
Syntax: snmp-server agent-port <PORT>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server community command do?

The snmp-server community command adds an SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. A community string is a password that controls read access to the SNMP agent. A network management program must supply this name when attempting to get SNMP information from the switch. A maximum of 10 community strings are supported. Once you create your own community string, the default community string (public) is deleted. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. When no community string exists, a default community string with the value public is automatically defined. Syntax: snmp-server community <STRING>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server community command?
Syntax: snmp-server community <STRING>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server host command do?
The snmp-server host command is documented in this guide. Syntax: snmp-server host <IPv4-ADDR | IPv6-ADDR> trap version <VERSION> [community <STRING>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server host command?
Syntax: snmp-server host <IPv4-ADDR | IPv6-ADDR> trap version <VERSION> [community <STRING>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server system-contact command do?
The snmp-server system-contact command sets SNMP contact information. The no form of this command removes the SNMP contact information. Syntax: snmp-server system-contact <INFO>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server system-contact command?
Syntax: snmp-server system-contact <INFO>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server system-location command do?
The snmp-server system-location command sets the SNMP location information. The no form of this command removes the SNMP location information. Syntax: snmp-server system-location <INFO>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server system-location command?
Syntax: snmp-server system-location <INFO>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server vrf command do?

The snmp-server vrf command configures a VRF on which the SNMP agent listens for incoming requests. By default, the SNMP agent does not listen on any VRF. 6100 only supports default VRF. The no form of this command stops the SNMP agent from listening for incoming requests on the specified VRF. Syntax: snmp-server vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server vrf command?
Syntax: snmp-server vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>] command do?

The snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>] command creates an SNMP v3 context on the specified VRF. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP context. Configures the SNMP v3 SNMP engine-id allowing an administrator to configure a unique SNMP engine-id for the switch. This engine-id is used by the NMS management tool to identify and distinguish multiple switches on the same network. The no form of this command restores the default engine-id, created by the switch using a combination of the enterprise OID value and the switch's MAC address. Configures the SNMP v3 security level. The security level determines which SMNPv3 users defined by the command snmpv3 user are able to connect. The no form of this command changes the security level as follows:. Syntax: snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>] command?
Syntax: snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure CoPP?

To configure CoPP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure the default CoPP policy, edit an existing policy, or create a policy with the command copp- 2. Add, edit, or remove classes in the policy with the command class 3. If the policy is not the active policy on the switch, apply it with the command apply copp-policy 4. Review the CoPP policy configuration settings with the command show copp-policy. Commands shown in the procedure include: policy..
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the apply copp-policy command do?

The apply copp-policy command applies a CoPP policy to the switch, replacing the policy that is in effect. There may be a brief interruption in traffic flow to the management processor while the switch implements the change. Enter the no apply copp-policy <NAME> command with the name of a CoPP policy to unapply a CoPP policy and apply the default CoPP policy. This will only take effect if the specified policy is actively applied. Since there must always be a CoPP policy applied, this command effectively attempts to replace the applied CoPP policy with the default CoPP policy. The default CoPP policy cannot be unapplied using this command. Syntax: apply copp-policy { <NAME> | default }.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the apply copp-policy command?
Syntax: apply copp-policy { <NAME> | default }
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the class command?
Syntax: class <CLASS> {drop | priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE>}
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear copp-policy statistics command do?
The clear copp-policy statistics command resets statistics for all CoPP classes to zero. Syntax: clear copp-policy statistics.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear copp-policy statistics command?
Syntax: clear copp-policy statistics
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> command do?

The default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> command configures the default class that is automatically defined for all CoPP policies. The default class cannot be removed, but its configuration can be changed. The default class is applied to traffic that does not match any other class defined for a policy. Syntax: default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> command?
Syntax: default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show copp-policy factory-default command do?
The show copp-policy factory-default command display the configuration for the factory-default CoPP policy. Syntax: show copp-policy factory-default [commands].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show copp-policy factory-default command?
Syntax: show copp-policy factory-default [commands]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show copp-policy statistics command do?
The show copp-policy statistics command displays statistics for all classes, or a single class, in the active CoPP policy. Syntax: show copp-policy statistics [class <CLASS> | default-class].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show copp-policy statistics command?
Syntax: show copp-policy statistics [class <CLASS> | default-class]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the portfilter <INTERFACE-LIST> command do?

The portfilter <INTERFACE-LIST> command configures the specified ports so they do not egress any packets that were received on the source port specified in interface context. Syntax: portfilter <INTERFACE-LIST>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the portfilter <INTERFACE-LIST> command?
Syntax: portfilter <INTERFACE-LIST>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show portfilter command do?
The show portfilter command displays filter settings for all interfaces or a specific interface. Syntax: show portfilter [<IFNAME>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show portfilter command?
Syntax: show portfilter [<IFNAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is DNS?

DNS: The Domain Name System (DNS) is the Internet protocol for mapping a hostname to its IP address. DNS allows users to enter more readily memorable and intuitive hostnames, rather than IP addresses, to identify devices connected to a network. It also allows a host to keep the same hostname even if it changes its IP address. Hostname resolution can be either static or dynamic. • In static resolution, a local table is defined on the switch that associates hostnames with their IP addresses. Static tables can be used to speed up the resolution of frequently queried hosts. • Dynamic resolution requires that the switch query a DNS server located elsewhere on the network. Dynamic name resolution takes more time than static name resolution, but requires far less configuration and management. DNS client The DNS client resolves hostnames to IP addresses for protocols that are running on the switch. When the DNS client receives a request to resolve a hostname, it can do so in one of two ways: • Forward the request to a DNS name server for resolution.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip dns domain-list command do?

The ip dns domain-list command configures one or more domain names that are appended to the DNS request. The DNS client appends each name in succession until the DNS server replies. Domains can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. The no form of this command removes a domain from the list. Syntax: ip dns domain-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [vrf default].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip dns domain-list command?
Syntax: ip dns domain-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [vrf default]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip dns domain-name command do?

The ip dns domain-name command configures a domain name that is appended to the DNS request. The domain can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. If a domain list is defined with the command ip dns domain-list, the domain name defined with this command is ignored. The no form of this command removes the domain name. Syntax: ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip dns domain-name command?
Syntax: ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip dns host command do?

The ip dns host command associates a static IP address with a hostname. The DNS client returns this IP address instead of querying a DNS server for an IP address for the hostname. Up to six hosts can be defined. If no VRF is defined, the default VRF is used. The no form of this command removes a static IP address associated with a hostname. 142 AOS-CX 10.06 Fundamentals Guide. Syntax: ip dns host <HOST-NAME> <IP-ADDR> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip dns host command?
Syntax: ip dns host <HOST-NAME> <IP-ADDR> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip dns command do?
The show ip dns command shows all DNS client configuration settings or the settings for a specific VRF. Syntax: show ip dns [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip dns command?
Syntax: show ip dns [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Device discovery and configuration?

Device discovery and configuration: Device profiles NOTE: Device profiles rely on role configurations. For information on role configurations, see the Security Guide. Device profiles are used to dynamically assign port attributes based on the type of devices connected, without having to create a RADIUS infrastructure. You can map device profiles to device groups. A device group contains various match criteria, which can be obtained from multiple sources, such as LLDP, CDP, and local MAC match. Device profiles contain port attributes to be assigned to the port when a connected device matches a device group. Device profiles are supported on different scenarios. It can be applied on interfaces that are configured with security (802.1X or MAC authentication), or applied based on L2 port (LLDP, CDP), or applied on standalone ports with the block-until-profile-applied command enabled. All the methods are mutually exclusive of each other.

section: Configuring a device profile for LLDP

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure a device profile for LLDP?

To configure a device profile for LLDP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Create an LLDP group with the command port-access lldp-group 2. Define rules for adding devices to an LLDP group with the command match 3. Define rules for ignoring devices so that they are not added to an LLDP group with the command 4. Create a device profile with the command port-access device-profile 5. Add the LLDP group with the command associate lldp-group 6. Add a role to a device profile with the command associate role. Make sure that the role is already 7. Enable the device profile with the command enable.

task: Configuring a device profile for LLDP

section: Configuring a device profile for CDP

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure a device profile for CDP?

To configure a device profile for CDP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Create a CDP group with the command port-access cdp-group 2. Define rules for adding devices to a CDP group with the command match 3. Define rules for ignoring devices so that they are not added to a CDP group with the command ignore 4. Create a device profile with the command port-access device-profile 5. Add a CDP group to a device profile with the command associate cdp-group 6. Add a role to a device profile with the command associate role. Make sure that the role is already 7. Enable a device profile with the command enable.

task: Configuring a device profile for CDP

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa authentication port-access allow-lldp-bpdu command do?

The aaa authentication port-access allow-lldp-bpdu command allows all packets related to the LLDP BPDU (Bridge Protocol Data Unit) on a secure port. The no form of this command blocks the LLDP BPDU on a secure port. On a nonsecure port, the command has no effect. 148 AOS-CX 10.06 Fundamentals Guide. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access allow-lldp-bpdu.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access allow-lldp-bpdu command?
Syntax: aaa authentication port-access allow-lldp-bpdu
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the associate cdp-group <GROUP-NAME> command do?

The associate cdp-group <GROUP-NAME> command associates a CDP (Cisco Discovery Protocol) group with a device profile. A maximum of two CDP groups can be associated with a device profile. The no form of this command removes a CDP group from a device profile. Associates an LLDP group with a device profile. A maximum of two LLDP groups can be associated with a device profile The no form of this command removes an LLDP group from a device profile. Associates a MAC group with a device profile. A maximum of two MAC groups can be associated with a device profile. The no form of this command removes a MAC group from a device profile. Syntax: associate cdp-group <GROUP-NAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the associate cdp-group <GROUP-NAME> command?
Syntax: associate cdp-group <GROUP-NAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the associate role <ROLE-NAME> command do?

The associate role <ROLE-NAME> command associates a role with a device profile. Only one role can be associated with a device profile. For information on how to configure a role, see the port access role information in the Security Guide. The no form of this command removes a role from a device profile. Disables a device profile. The no form of this command enables a device profile. Enables a device profile. The no form of this command disables a device profile. Syntax: associate role <ROLE-NAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the associate role <ROLE-NAME> command?
Syntax: associate role <ROLE-NAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the mac-group <MAC-GROUP-NAME> command do?

The mac-group <MAC-GROUP-NAME> command creates a MAC group or modifies an existing MAC group. A MAC group is used to classify connected devices based on the MAC address details, such as mask or OUI. A maximum of 32 MAC groups can be configured on the switch. A maximum of 2 MAC groups can be associated with a device profile. Each group accepts 64 match or ignore commands. The no form of this command removes a MAC group. Syntax: mac-group <MAC-GROUP-NAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the mac-group <MAC-GROUP-NAME> command?
Syntax: mac-group <MAC-GROUP-NAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the port-access cdp-group <CDP-GROUP-NAME> command do?

The port-access cdp-group <CDP-GROUP-NAME> command creates a CDP (Cisco Discovery Protocol) group or modifies an existing CDP group. A CDP Group is used to classify connected devices based on the CDP packet details advertised by the device. A maximum of 32 CDP groups can be configured on the switch. Each group accepts 64 match/ignore commands. The no form of this command removes a CDP group. Syntax: port-access cdp-group <CDP-GROUP-NAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the port-access cdp-group <CDP-GROUP-NAME> command?
Syntax: port-access cdp-group <CDP-GROUP-NAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the port-access device-profile <DEVICE-PROFILE-NAME> command do?

The port-access device-profile <DEVICE-PROFILE-NAME> command creates a new device profile and switches to the config-device-profile context. A maximum of 32 device profiles can be created. The no form of this command removes a device profile. Configures the switch to block the port until a profile match occurs for a device. This configuration is required when no security feature is enabled on the port. You must enable this mode or security on the port for local MAC match feature to operate. You must not enable both features on the same port at the same time. 168 AOS-CX 10.06 Fundamentals Guide NOTE: You must not combine any other AAA configurations with the block-until-profile-applied mode. The no form of this command removes a rule for adding devices to a MAC group. Creates an LLDP group or modifies an existing LLDP group. Syntax: port-access device-profile <DEVICE-PROFILE-NAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the port-access device-profile <DEVICE-PROFILE-NAME> command?
Syntax: port-access device-profile <DEVICE-PROFILE-NAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear lldp neighbors command do?
The clear lldp neighbors command clears all LLDP neighbor details. Syntax: clear lldp neighbors.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear lldp neighbors command?
Syntax: clear lldp neighbors
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear lldp statistics command do?
The clear lldp statistics command clears all LLDP neighbor statistics. Syntax: clear lldp statistics.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear lldp statistics command?
Syntax: clear lldp statistics
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lldp command do?

The lldp command enables LLDP support globally on all active interfaces. By default, LLDP is enabled. The no form of this command disables LLDP support globally on all active interfaces. It does not remove any LLDP configuration settings. Sets the 802.3 TLVs to be advertised. By default, advertisement of both POE and MAC/PHY TLVs is enabled. The no form of this command disables advertisement of 802.3 TLVs. Sets the holdtime that is used to calculate the LLDP Time-to-Live value. Time-to-Live defines the length of time that neighbors consider LLDP information sent by this agent as valid. When Time-to-Live expires, the information is deleted by the neighbor. Time-to-live is calculated by multiplying holdtime by the value of lldp timer. The no form of this command sets the holdtime to its default value of 4. Syntax: lldp.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the lldp command?
Syntax: lldp
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lldp med-location {civic-addr | elin-addr } command do?

The lldp med-location {civic-addr | elin-addr } command configures support for the LLDP-MED TLV. Supports only civic address and emergency location information number (ELIN). Coordinate-based location is not supported. The no form of this command disables support for the LLDP MED TLV. Enables reception of LLDP information on an interface. By default, LLDP reception is enabled on all active interfaces. The no form of this command disables reception of LLDP information on an interface. Sets the amount of time (in seconds) to wait before performing LLDP initialization on an interface. The no form of this command sets the reinitialization time to its default value of 2 seconds. Syntax: lldp med-location {civic-addr | elin-addr }.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the lldp med-location {civic-addr | elin-addr } command?
Syntax: lldp med-location {civic-addr | elin-addr }
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lldp select-tlv <TLV-NAME> command do?

The lldp select-tlv <TLV-NAME> command selects a TLV that the LLDP agent will send and receive. By default, all supported TLVs are sent and received. The no form of this command stops the LLDP agent from sending and receiving a specific TLV. Sets the interval (in seconds) at which local LLDP information is updated and TLVs are sent to neighboring network devices by the LLDP agent. The minimum setting for this timer must be four times the value of lldp txdelay. For example, this is a valid configuration: • lldp timer = 16 • lldp txdelay = 4 And, this is an invalid configuration: • lldp timer = 5 • lldp txdelay = 2 NOTE: When copying a saved configuration to the running configuration, the value for lldp timer is applied before the value of lldp txdelay. Syntax: lldp select-tlv <TLV-NAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the lldp select-tlv <TLV-NAME> command?
Syntax: lldp select-tlv <TLV-NAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp local-device command do?

The show lldp local-device command shows global LLDP information advertised by the switch, as well as port-based data. If VLANs are configured on any active interfaces, the VLAN ID is only shown for trunk native or untagged VLAN IDs on access interfaces. Syntax: show lldp local-device.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp local-device command?
Syntax: show lldp local-device
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp neighbor-info command do?

The show lldp neighbor-info command displays information about neighboring devices for all interfaces or for a specific interface. The information displayed varies depending on the type of neighbor connected and the type of TLVs sent by the neighbor. Syntax: show lldp neighbor-info [<INTERFACE-NAME>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp neighbor-info command?
Syntax: show lldp neighbor-info [<INTERFACE-NAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp neighbor-info detail command do?
The show lldp neighbor-info detail command shows detailed LLDP neighbor information for all LLDP neighbor connected interfaces. Syntax: show lldp neighbor-info detail.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp neighbor-info detail command?
Syntax: show lldp neighbor-info detail
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp statistics command do?
The show lldp statistics command shows global LLDP statistics or statistics for a specific interface. Syntax: show lldp statistics [<INTERFACE-ID>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp statistics command?
Syntax: show lldp statistics [<INTERFACE-ID>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp tlv command do?
The show lldp tlv command shows the LLDP TLVs that are configured for send and receive. Syntax: show lldp tlv.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp tlv command?
Syntax: show lldp tlv
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear cdp counters command do?
The clear cdp counters command clears CDP counters. Syntax: clear cdp counters.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear cdp counters command?
Syntax: clear cdp counters
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear cdp neighbor-info command do?
The clear cdp neighbor-info command clears CDP neighbor information. Syntax: clear cdp neighbor-info.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear cdp neighbor-info command?
Syntax: clear cdp neighbor-info
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show cdp command do?
The show cdp command shows CDP information for all interfaces. Syntax: show cdp.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show cdp command?
Syntax: show cdp
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show cdp neighbor-info command do?
The show cdp neighbor-info command shows CDP information for all neighbors or for CDP information on a specific interface. Syntax: show cdp neighbor-info <INTERFACE-ID>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show cdp neighbor-info command?
Syntax: show cdp neighbor-info <INTERFACE-ID>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Zero Touch Provisioning?

Zero Touch Provisioning: (ZTP) enables the auto-configuration of factory default switches without a network administrator onsite. When a switch is booted from its factory default configuration, ZTP autoprovisions the switch by automatically downloading and installing a firmware file, a configuration file, or both. With ZTP, even a nontechnical user (for example: a store manager in a retail chain or a teacher in a school) can deploy devices at a site. ZTP support The switch supports standards-based Zero Touch Provisioning (ZTP) operations as follows: • The switch must be running the factory default configuration. • ZTP operations are supported over IPv4 connections only. IPv6 connections are not supported for ZTP operations. • You must configure the DHCP server to provide a standards-based ZTP server solution. Options and features that are specific to Network Management Solution (NMS) tools, such as AirWave, are not supported.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ztp information command do?
The show ztp information command shows information about Zero Touch Provisioning (ZTP) operations performed on the switch. Syntax: show ztp information.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ztp information command?
Syntax: show ztp information
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show usb file-system command do?

The show usb file-system command shows directory listings for a mounted USB device. When entered without the <PATH> parameter the top level directory tree is shown. Syntax: show usb file-system [<PATH>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show usb file-system command?
Syntax: show usb file-system [<PATH>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you accessing the AOS-CX Web UI?

To accessing the AOS-CX Web UI, follow the documented procedure: 1. Start a supported web browser and enter the IP address of the management port in the address bar. Use 2. Optionally a pre- and post-login message may be displayed. The message can be customized or disabled 3. At the login page, enter your user name and password credentials, then click Login 4. After you log in, the main Web UI page is displayed.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you troubleshoot Web UI access issues?

To troubleshoot Web UI access issues, follow the documented procedure: 1. Log out from one of the existing sessions 2. If the session cookie is lost and it is not possible to log out of the session, then wait for the session idle.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the VSX command do?

The VSX command shows the Aruba Virtual Switching Framework (VSF) configuration and status information. This link is displayed for the 6200 and 6300 switches that support VSF. Not applicable to the 6100 Switch Series.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface loopback command do?
The interface loopback command creates a loopback interface and enters loopback configuration mode. The no form of this command deletes a loopback interface. Syntax: interface loopback <INSTANCE>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface loopback command?
Syntax: interface loopback <INSTANCE>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 address command do?
The IPv6 address command sets the IPv6 address for a loopback interface. 14. Syntax: IPv6 address <IPv6-ADDR/MASK>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show interface loopback command?
Syntax: show interface loopback [brief | instance <ID>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route command do?

The ip route command adds an IPv4 static route on the default VRF. The no form of this command deletes a IPv4 static route. Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip route command?
Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route distance command do?

The ip route distance command configures the administrative distance for the IPv4 static route. The no form of this command deletes the static route. Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>] distance <VALUE>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip route distance command?
Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>] distance <VALUE>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route tag command do?
The ip route tag command configures tag for IPv4 static route. The no form of this command deletes tag for IPv4 static route. Syntax: ip route <A.B.C.D/M> [A.B.C.D | INTERFACE] tag <1-4294967295>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip route tag command?
Syntax: ip route <A.B.C.D/M> [A.B.C.D | INTERFACE] tag <1-4294967295>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 route command do?

The IPv6 route command adds an IPv6 static route. The no form of this command deletes an IPv6 static route on the default VRF. Syntax: IPv6 route <DEST-IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 route command?
Syntax: IPv6 route <DEST-IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 route tag command do?
The IPv6 route tag command configures tag for IPv6 static route. Syntax: IPv6 route <X:X::X:X/M> [X:X::X:X | INTERFACE] tag <1-4294967295>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 route tag command?
Syntax: IPv6 route <X:X::X:X/M> [X:X::X:X | INTERFACE] tag <1-4294967295>

section: Configuring OSPF on the routing switch

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure OSPF on the routing switch?

To configure OSPF on the routing switch, follow the documented procedure: 1. Create the OSPF instance and enter the OSPF router configuration context using the following command 2. Configure a global router ID using the following command. For command details, see router-id. Commands shown in the procedure include: router ospf <PROCESS-ID>; router-id <ROUTER-ADDRESS>.

task: Configuring OSPF on the routing switch

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary] command do?

The area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary] command sets the cost of the default route announced to NSSA or stub areas. The no form of this command resets the cost of the default route announced to NSSA or stub areas, to the default value of 1. Creates the NSSA area (Not So Stubby Area) with <AREA-ID> if not present. If area is present and not NSSA area, this command changes the area type to NSSA area. If no-summary is used, area type will be NSSA No- Summary. The no form of this command unsets the area type as NSSA. That is, the configured area will be changed to default normal area. The no area <AREA-ID> nssa no-summary command enables sending inter-area routes into NSSA, but will not unset the area as NSSA. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary] command?
Syntax: area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear ip ospf statistics command do?
The clear ip ospf statistics command clear the OSPF event statistics. Syntax: clear ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear ip ospf statistics command?
Syntax: clear ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default-information originate command do?

The default-information originate command configures OSPF to advertise the default route (0.0.0.0/0) to its neighbors if it is present in the routing table. The no form of this command disables advertisement of the default route. Configures OSPF to advertise the default route (0.0.0.0/0) to its neighbors, regardless if it is present in the routing table or not. The no form of this command sets the OSPF administrative distance to the default of 110. Syntax: default-information originate.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the default-information originate command?
Syntax: default-information originate
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default-metric <METRIC-VALUE> command do?

The default-metric <METRIC-VALUE> command sets the default metric for redistributed routes in the OSPF. The no form of this command sets the default metric to be used for redistributed routes into OSPF to the default of 25. Disables the OSPF process. IMPORTANT: This command does not remove the OSPF configurations. Syntax: default-metric <METRIC-VALUE>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the default-metric <METRIC-VALUE> command?
Syntax: default-metric <METRIC-VALUE>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the distance <DISTANCE> command do?

The distance <DISTANCE> command defines an administrative distance for OSPF. Administrative distance is used as a criteria to select the best route when multiple routes are present from different routing protocols. The no form of this command sets the OSPF administrative distance to the default of 110. Enables the OSPF process, if disabled. By default the OSPF process is enabled. 46. Syntax: distance <DISTANCE>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the distance <DISTANCE> command?
Syntax: distance <DISTANCE>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper} command do?

The graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper} command configures graceful restart parameters for OSPF. The no form of this command sets the restart interval to the default interval of 120 seconds or disables the helper mode, depending on the parameter specified. Syntax: graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper} command?
Syntax: graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper}
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf area command do?

The ip ospf area command runs the OSPF protocol on the interface with the configured IPv4 address for the area specified. The interfaces which have an IP address configured in this network or in a subset of this network, will participate in the OSPF protocol. To move an interface to a new area, unmap the existing area and then associate a new area with the interface. The no form of this command disables OSPF on the interface and removes the interface from the area. Interfaces which have an IP address configured on the network or in a subset of the network, stop participating in the OSPF protocol. Syntax: ip ospf <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf area command?
Syntax: ip ospf <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf authentication command do?

The ip ospf authentication command sets the authentication type that will be used for authentication with the neighbor router. The no form of this command deletes the authentication type used for a particular authentication with the neighbor router and sets to null authentication. Syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}.

syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf authentication command?
Syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}

syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf authentication-key command do?
The ip ospf authentication-key command is documented in this guide. Syntax: ip ospf authentication-key {ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf authentication-key command?
Syntax: ip ospf authentication-key {ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf cost command do?

The ip ospf cost command sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface. The interface cost is used as a parameter to calculate the best routes. The no form of this command sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface to the default cost 1. Syntax: ip ospf cost <INTERFACE-COST>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf cost command?
Syntax: ip ospf cost <INTERFACE-COST>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf dead-interval command do?

The ip ospf dead-interval command sets the interval after which a neighbor is declared dead if no hello packet is received on the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the interval after which a neighbor is declared dead, to the default for the OSPF interface. The default value is 40 seconds (generally 4 times the hello packet interval). Syntax: ip ospf dead-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf dead-interval command?
Syntax: ip ospf dead-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf hello-interval command do?

The ip ospf hello-interval command sets the time interval between OSPF hello packets for the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the time interval OSPF hello packets to the default of 10 seconds for the OSPF interface. Syntax: ip ospf hello-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf hello-interval command?
Syntax: ip ospf hello-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf keychain command do?

The ip ospf keychain command sets the key chain for md5 authentication. A key chain configures rotating keys for packet authenticating, reducing the risk of keys being compromised. The no form of this command deletes the key chain used for md5 authentication. Syntax: ip ospf keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf keychain command?
Syntax: ip ospf keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf network command do?

The ip ospf network command configures the OSPF network type for the interface. The no form of this command sets the network type for the interface to the system default which is broadcast network. Syntax: ip ospf network {broadcast | point-to-point}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf network command?
Syntax: ip ospf network {broadcast | point-to-point}
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf passive command do?

The ip ospf passive command configures the interface as an OSPF passive interface. With this setting, the interface participates in OSPF but does not send or receive packets on that interface. The no form of this command resets the interface as active. With this setting, the interface starts sending and receiving OSPF packets. Syntax: ip ospf passive.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf passive command?
Syntax: ip ospf passive
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf priority command do?

The ip ospf priority command sets the OSPF priority for the interface. The larger the numeric value of the priority, the higher the chances for it to become the designated router. Setting a priority of zero makes the router ineligible to become a designated router or back up designated router. The no form of this command sets the OSPF priority for the interface to the default of 1. Syntax: ip ospf priority <PRIORITY-VALUE>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf priority command?
Syntax: ip ospf priority <PRIORITY-VALUE>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf retransmit-interval command do?

The ip ospf retransmit-interval command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements for the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements to the default of 5 seconds for the OSPF interface. Syntax: ip ospf retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf retransmit-interval command?
Syntax: ip ospf retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf shutdown command do?

The ip ospf shutdown command disables OSPF on the interface. The interface state changes to Down. It does not remove the interface from the OSPF area. To remove the interface, use the command no ip ospf area. The no form of this command re-enables OSPF on the interface. Syntax: ip ospf shutdown.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf shutdown command?
Syntax: ip ospf shutdown
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf transit-delay command do?

The ip ospf transit-delay command sets the time delay in link state transmission for the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the delay in link state transmission to the default of 1 second for the OSPF interface. Syntax: ip ospf transit-delay <DELAY>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf transit-delay command?
Syntax: ip ospf transit-delay <DELAY>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME> command do?

The keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME> command sets the key chain for md5 authentication. A key chain configures rotating keys for packet authenticating, reducing the risk of keys being compromised. The no form of this command deletes the key chain used for md5 authentication. Syntax: keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME> command?
Syntax: keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]] command do?

The max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]] command sets the protocol to advertise a maximum metric so that other routers do not prefer this router as an intermediate hop in their shortest path first (SPF) calculations. If the on-startup parameter is used, the router is configured to advertise a maximum metric at startup for the time mentioned in seconds or for a default value of 600 seconds. The no form of this command advertises the normal cost metrics instead of advertising the maximized cost metric. This setting causes the router to be considered in traffic forwarding. Configures all OSPF interfaces as passive. The no form of this command sets all OSPF interfaces as active. Syntax: max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]] command?
Syntax: max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>] command do?

The redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>] command redistributes routes originating from other protocols, or from another OSPF process, to the current OSPF process. The no form of this command disables redistribution of routes to the current OSPF process. Syntax: redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>] command?
Syntax: redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH> command do?

The reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH> command sets the reference bandwidth for OSPFv2. If the OSPFv2 interface cost is not explicitly set. then the cost of all the OSPFv2 interfaces is recalculated based on the reference bandwidth and link speed of the interface. For VLAN interfaces the link speed value is taken as 1 Gbps (if the OSPFv2 interface cost is not explicitly set). The no form of this command sets the reference bandwidth for OSPF to the default of 100000 Mbps. Syntax: reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH> command?
Syntax: reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router ospf command do?

The router ospf command creates an OSPF process (if not created already) on a VRF, and switches to the OSPF router instance context. Up to eight OSPF processes are supported per VRF. The no form of this command removes the OSPF instance. Syntax: router ospf <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the router ospf command?
Syntax: router ospf <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router-id command do?

The router-id command sets an ID for the router in an IPv4 address format. The no form of this command unconfigures the router-id for the instance and sets the router-id to the default as follows: the router-id is selected dynamically as equal to the highest loopback address on the router, or the highest active interface if there are no loopback addresses. If no IP address is configured on any interfaces on the router, OSPF will not form an adjacency. Syntax: router-id <ROUTER-ADDR>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the router-id command?
Syntax: router-id <ROUTER-ADDR>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf command?
Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf interface command do?
The show ip ospf interface command displays general OSPF, area, state, and configuration information. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] interface [<interface-name>] [brief].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf interface command?
Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] interface [<interface-name>] [brief]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf lsdb command do?
The show ip ospf lsdb command is documented in this guide. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] lsdb [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf lsdb command?
Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] lsdb [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf routes command do?
The show ip ospf routes command is documented in this guide. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] routes.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf routes command?
Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] routes
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf statistics command do?
The show ip ospf statistics command displays OSPF statistics. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf statistics command?
Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command do?

The summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command summarizes the external routes with the matching address and mask. When advertising this route, its metric is set to the lowest cost path from among the routes that were summarized. The no form of this command disables route summarization. NOTE: This command only works for an ASBR (Autonomous System Boundary Router). Syntax: summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command?
Syntax: summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the timers lsa-arrival <DELAY> command do?

The timers lsa-arrival <DELAY> command configures the minimum delay between receiving the same LSA from a peer. The same LSA is an LSA that contains the same LSA ID number, LSA type, and advertising router ID. If an instance of the same LSA arrives sooner before the delay expires, the LSA is dropped. Generally, the LSA arrival timer should be set to a value less than or equal to the start-time value for the command timers throttle lsa start on the neighbor. The no form of this command sets the LSA timers to default values. Syntax: timers lsa-arrival <DELAY>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the timers lsa-arrival <DELAY> command?
Syntax: timers lsa-arrival <DELAY>

section: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME> command do?

The timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME> command configures the timers for LSA generation. The no form of this command sets the LSA timers to default values. Configures timers for SPF calculation. There are three timers:. Syntax: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>.

command: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>

Syntax: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>

section: Configuring external route redistribution and control

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure external route redistribution and control?

To configure external route redistribution and control, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable route redistribution using the following command and specify one of the following parameters: 2. Optionally, modify the default metric for redistribution using the following command. For command 3. Optionally, set the cost of default-summary LSAs using the following command. For command details, .

task: Configuring external route redistribution and control

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> command do?

The area <AREA-ID> command creates a normal area with <AREA-ID> set if not present. If area is present and is not the normal area, this command changes the area type to normal area. The no form of this command deletes the area with the <AREA-ID> specified. The area can be of any type (stub, stub no-summary, and default normal area). Syntax: area <AREA-ID>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> command?
Syntax: area <AREA-ID>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command do?

The area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command configures IPsec AH authentication for the specified area. OSPFv3 interfaces which have IPsec configured at the interface context will not use area level IPsec. The no form of this command removes IPsec AH authentication for the specified area. NOTE: IPsec is not supported for 6in6 tunnel interfaces. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command?
Syntax: area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command do?

The area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command configures IPsec ESP with authentication/encryption algorithm type and key for the specified area. OSPFv3 interfaces with IPsec configured at interface context will not use area level IPsec ESP configuration. The no form of this command removes IPsec ESP from the specified area. NOTE: IPsec is not supported for 6in6 tunnel interfaces. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command?
Syntax: area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command do?

The clear IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command clears the OSPFv3 event statistics. Sets the default metric for redistributed routes in the OSPFv3. Syntax: clear IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command?
Syntax: clear IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the disable command do?
The disable command disables the OSPFv3 process. By default OSPFv3 process is enabled. IMPORTANT: This command does not remove the OSPFv3 configurations. Syntax: disable.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the disable command?
Syntax: disable
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check} command do?

The graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check} command configures graceful restart for OSPFv3. By default graceful restart is enabled on the OSPFv3 router. The no form of this command sets the restart interval to the default of 120 seconds or disables helper mode depending on the specified parameters. Syntax: graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check} command?
Syntax: graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check}
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 area command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 area command runs the OSPFv3 protocol on the interface for the area specified. To move an interface to a new area, unmap the existing area and then associate a new area with the interface. The no form of this command disables OSPF on the interface and removes the interface from the area. Interfaces which have an IP address configured on the network or in a subset of the network, stop participating in the OSPF protocol. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 area command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null command do?
The IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null command is documented in this guide. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec command configures IPSec AH authentication. OSPFv3 interfaces that have IPsec configured at the interface context will not use area level IPsec. The no form of this command removes IPsec AH authentication for the specified area. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 cost command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 cost command sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface. The interface cost is used as a parameter to calculate the best routes. The no form of this command sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface to the default of 1. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 cost <INTERFACE-COST>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 cost command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 cost <INTERFACE-COST>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval command do?
The IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval command is documented in this guide. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec command configures IPsec ESP. OSPFv3 interfaces that have IPsec configured at the interface context will not use area level IPsec ESP. The no form of this command removes IPsec ESP for the specified area. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null command do?
The IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null command configures NULL ESP on an interface which disables IPsec ESP. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval command sets the time interval between OSPFv3 hello packets for the OSPFv3 interface. The no form of this command sets the time interval between OSPFv3 hello packets to the default for the OSPFv3 interface of 10 seconds. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 network command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 network command configures the network type for the interface. By default the network type is broadcast network. The no form of this command sets the network type for the interface to the system default of broadcast network. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 network {broadcast|point-to-point}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 network command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 network {broadcast|point-to-point}
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 passive command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 passive command configures the interface as an OSPFv3 passive interface. With this setting, the interface participates in the OSPF, but does not send or receive OSPF packets on that interface. The no form of this command resets the interface as active. With this setting, the interface starts sending and receiving OSPF packets. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 passive.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 passive command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 passive
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 priority command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 priority command sets the OSPFv3 priority for the interface. The larger the numeric value of the priority, the higher the chance it will become the designated router. Setting a priority of 0 makes the router ineligible to become a designated router or back up designated router. The no form of this command sets the OSPFv3 priority for the interface to the default of 1. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 priority <number-value>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 priority command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 priority <number-value>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements for the OSPFv3 interface. The no form of this command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements to the default 5 seconds. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown command disables OSPFv3 on the interface. The interface state changes to Down. It does not remove the interface from the OSPF area. To remove the interface, use the command no ip ospf area. The no form of this command re-enables OSPFv3 on the interface. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay command sets the time delay in Link state transmission for the OSPFv3 interface. The no form of this command sets the transit delay in Link state transmission to the default of 1 second. Sets the maximum number of ECMP routes that OSPFv3 can support. The no form of this command sets the maximum number of ECMP routes that OSPFv3 can support to the default value of 4. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay <DELAY>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay <DELAY>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>] command do?

The max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>] command sets the protocol to advertise a maximum metric so that other routers do not prefer the router as an intermediate hop in their shortest path first (SPF) calculations. If the on-startup parameter is used, the router is configured to advertise a maximum metric at startup. That is, for the time specified in seconds, or the default value of 600 seconds. To disable advertisement of the maximum metric, use the no form of the command. The no form of this command advertises the normal cost metrics instead of advertising the maximized cost metric. This setting causes the router to be considered in traffic forwarding. Syntax: max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>] command?
Syntax: max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the passive-interface default command do?

The passive-interface default command sets all OSPFv3 interfaces as passive. The no form of this command sets all the OSPFv3 interfaces as active. Redistributes routes originating from other protocols, or from another OSPF process, to the current OSPF process. The no form of this command disables redistribution of routes to the current OSPF process. Syntax: passive-interface default.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the passive-interface default command?
Syntax: passive-interface default
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the retransmit-interval <INTERVAL> command do?

The retransmit-interval <INTERVAL> command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements for virtual links. The no form of this command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements to the default of 5 seconds for virtual links. Syntax: retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the retransmit-interval <INTERVAL> command?
Syntax: retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router ospfv3 command do?

The router ospfv3 command creates the OSPFv3 process (if not created already) and enters the router OSPFv3 instance context. Optionally if specified, you can specify a named VRF, or the default VRF if the <vrf-name> is not specified. Only one OSPFv3 process is allowed per VRF. 116. Syntax: router ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].

Syntax: router ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]

The show IPv6 ospfv3 border-routers command shows the OSPFv3 routing table entries for Area Border Router (ABR) and Autonomous System Border Router (ASBR). Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] border-routers [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].

Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>] [brief]

The show IPv6 ospfv3 neighbors command shows information about OSPFv3 neighbors. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>].

Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 routes command do?
The show IPv6 ospfv3 routes command shows the OSPFv3 routing table information. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] routes [<PREFIX/LENGTH>].

Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] routes [<PREFIX/LENGTH>]

For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command?
Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics

Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command do?

The summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command summarizes the external routes with the matching address and mask. When advertising this route, its metric is set to the lowest cost path from among the routes that were summarized. The no form of this command disables route summarization. NOTE: This command only works for an ASBR (Autonomous System Boundary Router). Syntax: summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command?
Syntax: summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME> command do?

The timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME> command configures timers for SPF calculation. There are three timers: • start-time Is the initial delay before an SPF calculation is started. Default is 200 milliseconds. • hold-time Is the progressive backoff time to wait before next scheduled SPF calculation. Default is 1000 milliseconds. If a route change event occurs during this period, the value doubles until it reaches the max-wait-time. • max-wait-time Is the maximum time to wait before the next scheduled SPF calculation. Default is 5000 milliseconds. This is used to limit the SPF hold timer and also defines the time to be considered for which the OSPF LSDB has to be stable, after which the SPF throttle mechanism is reset. The no form of this command sets all the configured non-default timers to default value. Syntax: timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME> command?
Syntax: timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the transit-delay <DELAY> command do?

The transit-delay <DELAY> command sets the time delay in Link state transmission for virtual links. The no form of this command sets the delay in Link state transmission to the default of 1 second for virtual links. Enables the notification of the events to be sent as traps to the SNMP management stations for OSPFv3. The no form of this command disables the notification of the events to be sent as traps to the SNMP management stations for OSPFv3. 130. Syntax: transit-delay <DELAY>.

Syntax: transit-delay <DELAY>

The accept-lifetime [start-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>] {duration {<seconds> | infinite} | end-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>} command configures the duration for which the key is valid for receiving packets. messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the client track ip { enable | disable | auto } command do?

The client track ip { enable | disable | auto } command enables client IP address tracking on the specified set of interfaces. Tracking will take effect only if client IP address tracking is enabled at both the system level and for the VLAN to which the port belongs. Default: auto. The no form of the command disables client IP address tracking on the specified set of interfaces. Configures the maximum number of clients to be tracked on the specified set of interfaces. For the 6100 Switch Series the no form of the command resets the client limit to the default value of 128. Syntax: client track ip { enable | disable | auto }.
