messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator max-eapol-requests command do?

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator max-eapol-requests command configures the number of EAPOL requests to send to a supplicant that must time out before authentication fails and the authentication session ends. The no form of the command resets the maximum number of EAPOL requests to the default, 5. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator max-eapol-requests <MAX-EAPOL-REQUESTS>.

section: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator quiet-period

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator quiet-period command do?

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator quiet-period command configures the period during which the port does not try to acquire a supplicant. This period begins after the last authentication attempt, authorized by the maximum retries parameter, fails. You can configure the number of maximum retries with the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator max-retries command. The no form of the command resets the quiet period to the default, 60 seconds. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator quiet-period <PERIOD>.

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator reauth-period command do?

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator reauth-period command configures the period after which the authenticated clients are reauthenticated on the port. You must enable reauthentication on the port before configuring the reauthentication period. The no form of the command resets the reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth command enables or disables MAC authentication globally or at the port-level. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth {enable | disable}.

For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth-method command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth-method command configures the RADIUS authentication method for MAC authentication. Following are the MAC authentication methods supported: • CHAP • PAP. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth-method {chap | pap}.

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth-period command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth-period command configures the period during which an authenticated client, which has failed to reauthenticate because the RADIUS server is unreachable, remains authenticated. The no form of the command resets the cached reauthentication period to the default, 30 seconds. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth-period <PERIOD>.

For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth password command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth password command enables and configures the global password that the switch must use for MAC authentication. The password can be either in ciphertext or plaintext format. The no form of the command disables the password for MAC authentication. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth password {plaintext|ciphertext}<PASSWORD>.

For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet-period command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet-period command configures the period during which the switch does not try to authenticate a rejected client. The no form of the command resets the quiet period to the default, 60 seconds. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet-period <PERIOD>.

Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet-period <PERIOD>

The clear mac-auth statistics command clears the MAC authentication statistics associated with the port and all the authenticator state machines associated to this port. If no interface is specified, the statistics is cleared for all MAC authentication enabled ports. Syntax: clear mac-auth statistics [interface <IF-NAME>].

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the port-access policy <POLICY-NAME> command do?

The port-access policy <POLICY-NAME> command creates or modifies policy and policy entries. A policy is made up of one or more policy entries ordered and prioritized by sequence numbers. Each entry has an IPv4/IPv6 class and one or more policy actions associated with it. 232. Syntax: port-access policy <POLICY-NAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the port-access policy <POLICY-NAME> command?
Syntax: port-access policy <POLICY-NAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear port-access policy hitcounts command do?
The clear port-access policy hitcounts command is documented in this guide. Syntax: clear port-access policy <POLICY-NAME> hitcounts {port | client}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear port-access policy hitcounts command?
Syntax: clear port-access policy <POLICY-NAME> hitcounts {port | client}
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show port-access policy command do?
The show port-access policy command is documented in this guide. Syntax: show port-access policy [<POLICY-NAME>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show port-access policy command?
Syntax: show port-access policy [<POLICY-NAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what security policy information is documented for ACLs applied on a VLAN?

ACLs applied on a VLAN: If an ACL rule is applied on a pre-auth role VLAN, traffic entering that VLAN will be affected. Traffic will be affected based on the rule in the ACL. Rate-limiting on an interface If the traffic is rate-limited on an interface as part of a pre-auth role VLAN, the traffic will be impacted. The traffic will be affected based on the rule in the rate-limiting configuration command. Authenticated or rejected clients Clients that are authenticated or rejected by the RADIUS server are given different VLANs. These clients are moved from pre-auth role to new VLANs based on the authentication by the RADIUS server. MAC pinning Clients whose MAC addresses are pinned and have undergone authentication will always be treated as authenticated. Pre-auth role VLAN is not applicable in this scenario. Effect of RADIUS tracking on pre-auth role If RADIUS tracking is enabled and no RADIUS server is available for authentication, the port will be changed from a pre-auth role VLAN to a critical VLAN.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the associate policy <POLICY-NAME> command do?
The associate policy <POLICY-NAME> command associates the policy with the current role. The no form of this command dissociates the policy from the role. Syntax: associate policy <POLICY-NAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the associate policy <POLICY-NAME> command?
Syntax: associate policy <POLICY-NAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the auth-mode {client-mode | device-mode} command do?
The auth-mode {client-mode | device-mode} command configures the authentication mode for the clients that are associated with the current role. Syntax: auth-mode {client-mode | device-mode}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the auth-mode {client-mode | device-mode} command?
Syntax: auth-mode {client-mode | device-mode}
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the client-inactivity timeout {<CLIENT-INACTIVITY-PERIOD> | none} command do?

The client-inactivity timeout {<CLIENT-INACTIVITY-PERIOD> | none} command enables cached reauthentication, setting the period after which clients that associated with the current role must be reauthenticated. The no form of this command disables cached authentication. Configures the period that the switch waits for a response from a client after which it removes the client from the role. The no form of the command resets the timeout period to the default. Syntax: client-inactivity timeout {<CLIENT-INACTIVITY-PERIOD> | none}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the client-inactivity timeout {<CLIENT-INACTIVITY-PERIOD> | none} command?
Syntax: client-inactivity timeout {<CLIENT-INACTIVITY-PERIOD> | none}
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the poe-priority {critical | high | low} command do?

The poe-priority {critical | high | low} command configures the power distribution priority for the port access roles. High power consumption can be prevented using poe-priority control mechanism. The no form of this command restores the power distribution to its default priority. Syntax: poe-priority {critical | high | low}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the poe-priority {critical | high | low} command?
Syntax: poe-priority {critical | high | low}
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the port-access role <ROLE-NAME> command do?

The port-access role <ROLE-NAME> command creates a new port access role or modifies an existing role. This command takes you into the config-pa- role context. A maximum of 32 port access roles can be created. The no form of this command deletes a role. Configures the period after which clients that associated with the current role must be reauthenticated. NOTE: The reauthentication period configured here takes precedence over the reauthentication period configured at the port level. Syntax: port-access role <ROLE-NAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the port-access role <ROLE-NAME> command?
Syntax: port-access role <ROLE-NAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show port-access role command do?
The show port-access role command shows information about roles configured locally, or downloaded from RADIUS server. Syntax: show port-access role {local | radius | name <ROLE-NAME>}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show port-access role command?
Syntax: show port-access role {local | radius | name <ROLE-NAME>}
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the stp-admin-edge-port command do?

The stp-admin-edge-port command configures the port as a spanning tree administrative edge port for the role. This configuration removes the port participation from STP interactions when onboarding devices. This in turn helps in faster onboarding of devices. The no form of the command disables STP edge port functionality. NOTE: If the port receives STP BPDU on the STP administrative edge configured port, the port will move to the STP state. You must configure the port as an STP administrative edge port only if you are sure that the connected device will not participate in STP interactions. Configures QoS trust mode for the role. The no form of this command configures the default trust mode for the role. Syntax: stp-admin-edge-port.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the stp-admin-edge-port command?
Syntax: stp-admin-edge-port
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vlan command do?

The vlan command configures VLAN IDs or VLAN names, and VLAN modes for a port access role. You can configure either VLAN IDs or VLAN names, or a combination of both for a role. The no form of the command deletes the VLAN configuration from the role. For trunk allowed VLAN names, you can delete the VLAN names individually or all names at once. Syntax: vlan {access | trunk native | trunk allowed} <VLAN-ID>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vlan command?
Syntax: vlan {access | trunk native | trunk allowed} <VLAN-ID>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what security policy information is documented for trunk allowed <VLAN-ID>?

trunk allowed <VLAN-ID>: Specifies the list of tagged or allowed VLANs on the trunk interface. Supports a list of VLAN IDs in the range 1 to 4094. The 6300, 6400 Switch Series support a maximum of 1024 trunk allowed VLAN IDs. The 6200 Switch Series supports a maximum of 256 trunk allowed VLAN IDs. 258.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what security policy information is documented for trunk allowed name <VLAN-NAME>?

trunk allowed name <VLAN-NAME>: Specifies the tagged or allowed VLAN name on the trunk interface. Supports a single VLAN name in each CLI with a maximum length of 32 characters. The switch supports a maximum of 50 trunk allowed VLAN names. Authority Administrators or local user group members with execution rights for this command. Usage Note the following points when configuring the VLAN IDs and names for a role: • • For VLAN access and VLAN trunk native respectively, it is recommended to configure only one of either VLAN ID or name for a role. In case both VLAN ID and name are configured, then VLAN ID takes precedence and is applied with the role. For VLAN trunk allowed, you can collectively configure a maximum of 50 names and 1024 VLAN IDs on the 6300, 6400 Switch Series, or 256 VLAN IDs on the 6200 Switch Series. In case this limit is exceeded in the role, then that role is rejected when applying it to an onboarding device.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the port-access port-security {enable | disable} command do?

The port-access port-security {enable | disable} command enables or disables port security globally or at the port level. Configures the maximum number of clients that are allowed on a port. After configuring the maximum clients limit, the MAC addresses of the clients can be learned by one of the following methods: 268. Syntax: port-access port-security {enable | disable}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the port-access port-security {enable | disable} command?
Syntax: port-access port-security {enable | disable}
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the port-access port-security mac-address <MAC-ADDRESS> command do?

The port-access port-security mac-address <MAC-ADDRESS> command configures the MAC addresses of static clients. The no form of this command removes an authorized static client from the port. Syntax: port-access port-security mac-address <MAC-ADDRESS>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the port-access port-security mac-address <MAC-ADDRESS> command?
Syntax: port-access port-security mac-address <MAC-ADDRESS>

The show port-access port-security interface client-status command shows port security clients status information for the ports. The output can be filtered by interface or MAC address. Syntax: show port-access port-security interface {all|<IF-NAME>}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show port-access port-security interface client-status command?
Syntax: show port-access port-security interface {all|<IF-NAME>}

The show port-access port-security interface port-statistics command shows port security statistics for the ports in a switch. The output can be filtered by interface. Syntax: show port-access port-security interface {all|<IF-NAME>} port-statistics.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show port-access port-security interface port-statistics command?
Syntax: show port-access port-security interface {all|<IF-NAME>} port-statistics
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the fault-monitor profile <profile-name> command do?

The fault-monitor profile <profile-name> command creates a fault monitoring profile. You can create maximum of 16 fault monitoring profiles to monitor different types of interfaces. The no form of this command deletes the fault monitoring profile. Syntax: fault-monitor profile <profile-name>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the fault-monitor profile <profile-name> command?
Syntax: fault-monitor profile <profile-name>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the all | excessive-oversize-packets | excessive-jabbers | excessive-fragments command do?

The all | excessive-oversize-packets | excessive-jabbers | excessive-fragments command enables the faults for a profile. NOTE: If user has not configured threshold, action or auto-enable parameters, then this command enables fault with the default values. By default, the faults are disabled in a profile. Configuring threshold, action, or auto-enable will not enable the fault. The no form of this command disables the faults for a profile. Syntax: all | excessive-oversize-packets | excessive-jabbers | excessive-fragments.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the all | excessive-oversize-packets | excessive-jabbers | excessive-fragments command?
Syntax: all | excessive-oversize-packets | excessive-jabbers | excessive-fragments
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the excessive-jabbers | excessive-crc-errors | excessive-oversize-packets command do?

The excessive-jabbers | excessive-crc-errors | excessive-oversize-packets command configures and changes the fault threshold value for the profile. The no form of this command sets the threshold to its default value. Syntax: excessive-jabbers | excessive-crc-errors | excessive-oversize-packets.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the excessive-jabbers | excessive-crc-errors | excessive-oversize-packets command?
Syntax: excessive-jabbers | excessive-crc-errors | excessive-oversize-packets

The show fault-monitor profile command shows the fault monitoring profile information. The command without the profile name displays all the available fault monitoring profiles. Syntax: show fault-monitor profile.

Syntax: show fault-monitor profile
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show interface fault-monitor profile command do?

The show interface fault-monitor profile command shows the fault monitoring profile configured for the particular interface, range of interfaces, or all the interfaces. Syntax: show interface [<IF-NAME>|<IF-RANGE>] fault-monitor profile.

Syntax: show interface [<IF-NAME>|<IF-RANGE>] fault-monitor profile

The show interface fault-monitor status command shows the active fault monitoring profile configured for the particular interface, range of interfaces, or all the interfaces. Syntax: show interface [<IF-NAME>|<IF-RANGE>] fault-monitor status.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show interface fault-monitor status command?
Syntax: show interface [<IF-NAME>|<IF-RANGE>] fault-monitor status

The show running-config all command shows the running configuration with all options for the fault monitoring profile. Syntax: show running-config all.

For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what security policy information is documented for Auditors and auditing tasks?

Auditors and auditing tasks: The auditors group enables administrators to create users that can perform auditing tasks without allowing those users the authority to view or change the switch configuration. As is the case for other users, auditors can access the switch using the Web UI, REST API, or the CLI. Auditing tasks (CLI) When you log on to the switch CLI as a user with auditor rights, you have access to the auditor command context only. auditor> The tasks that can be performed by auditors are as follows. The commands listed are the only commands auditors can execute other than session commands like print, list, and exit. However, auditors can use all command options except as noted. See the command description for each command for complete information about the command. Task.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the event-trap-enable command do?

The event-trap-enable command enables the notification of events to be sent as traps to the SNMP management stations. It is enabled by default. The no form of this command disables the event traps. Syntax: event-trap-enable.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the event-trap-enable command?
Syntax: event-trap-enable
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server historical-counters-monitor command do?

The snmp-server historical-counters-monitor command enables the Remote Network Monitoring agent (rmond) to start collecting historical interface statistics. The no form of this command stops the historical interface statistics collection. Syntax: snmp-server historical-counters-monitor.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server historical-counters-monitor command?
Syntax: snmp-server historical-counters-monitor
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server trap-source interface vrf command?
Syntax: snmp-server trap-source {interface <IF-NAME> | <IPv4-Address> | <IPv6-Address>} [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID> command do?

The snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID> command configures the SNMP v3 SNMP engine-id allowing an administrator to configure a unique SNMP engine-id for the switch. This engine-id is used by the NMS management tool to identify and distinguish multiple switches on the same network. The no form of this command restores the default engine-id, created by the switch using a combination of the enterprise OID value and the switch's MAC address. Configures the SNMP v3 security level. The security level determines which SMNPv3 users defined by the command snmpv3 user are able to connect. The no form of this command changes the security level as follows: 24 AOS-CX 10.06 SNMP/MIB Guide • no snmpv3 security-level auth: Sets the security level to auth-privacy. Syntax: snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID> command?
Syntax: snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Entity MIB support?

Entity MIB support: The Entity MIB, rfc 6933, allows network managers to retrieve physical containment and logical relationships for devices in the network. The entconfig Change trap is sent to configured SNMP-server hosts when a change occurs. The trap is configured to send notifications no more than once every 5 seconds. We will be supporting the Entity MIB for read-only. Physical components that are supported include: • Chassis • • • • Fabric cards Fan trays Fans Line cards and their interfaces.
